content:
Value for Money: Are Burnley's Biggest Contracts Delivering?

Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council's spending records show a total expenditure of £355.2M across five financial years, from 2021/22 to 2025/26.
  • The top five suppliers account for a significant portion of this spend, totalling £136.7M, which represents 38.5% of the overall budget.
  • The single largest supplier, LIBERATA UK LTD, received £36.7M, making up 10.3% of the council's total analysed spending.
  • More than half of the council's expenditure, £184.4M or 51.9%, was directed to just the top ten suppliers.
  • The remaining 4,475 suppliers shared £170.8M, which is 48.1% of the total spend.

Burnley's Spending Landscape

Burnley Borough Council has overseen a substantial expenditure of £355.2M over the five financial years from 2021/22 to 2025/26. This significant sum was distributed among 4,485 unique suppliers. Understanding how this money is allocated is crucial for residents to assess value for money and transparency in local government spending.

The data reveals a concentrated pattern of spending. A small number of major contractors receive a disproportionately large share of the council's budget. The top ten suppliers alone account for £184.4M of the total spend, representing 51.9%. In contrast, the remaining 4,475 suppliers collectively received £170.8M, or 48.1% of the budget. This concentration raises questions about the scope and nature of the services provided by these key contractors.

Key Contractors and Their Roles

The five largest suppliers to Burnley Borough Council received a combined total of £136.7M, which is 38.5% of the entire analysed spend. This highlights the council's reliance on a select group of organisations for a substantial portion of its services and projects. The top three suppliers by expenditure are LIBERATA UK LTD, MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS LTD, and BARNFIELD INVESTMENT PROPERTIES LTD, each receiving tens of millions of pounds.

To provide a clearer picture of this concentration, the table below details the top ten suppliers, their total spend, and their percentage of the overall budget:

Rank Supplier Amount % of Total
1 LIBERATA UK LTD £36.7M 10.3%
2 MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS LTD £30.8M 8.7%
3 BARNFIELD INVESTMENT PROPERTIES LTD £29.4M 8.3%
4 GELDARDS £20.3M 5.7%
5 URBASER LTD £19.5M 5.5%
6 LANCASHIRE CC - PRESTON COUNTY HALL £16.8M 4.7%
7 BURNLEY LEISURE £12.9M 3.6%
8 FCC ENVIRONMENT/URBASER LTD £9.0M 2.5%
9 HISTORIC PROPERTY RESTORATION LTD £4.6M 1.3%
10 BEACHCROFT LLP £4.4M 1.2%

Examining High-Value Engagements

The largest single recipient of Burnley Borough Council funds is LIBERATA UK LTD, with an expenditure of £36.7M, accounting for 10.3% of the total spend. Liberata is known for providing business process outsourcing and IT services to local authorities. Such a substantial contract suggests a wide range of services, potentially including financial management, HR, or customer services. The scale of this contract warrants close scrutiny to ensure it delivers optimal efficiency and cost savings for the council and its taxpayers.

Following closely are property development and investment firms. MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS LTD received £30.8M, representing 8.7% of the total spend. BARNFIELD INVESTMENT PROPERTIES LTD was paid £29.4M, or 8.3%. These figures indicate significant investment in regeneration projects, property acquisition, or development initiatives within the borough. These projects often involve long-term commitments and substantial public funds, making their financial oversight critical. Legal services also feature prominently, with GELDARDS receiving £20.3M (5.7%) and BEACHCROFT LLP receiving £4.4M (1.2%).

Waste management services are also a major expenditure. URBASER LTD received £19.5M, which is 5.5% of the total. Another entity, FCC ENVIRONMENT/URBASER LTD, received an additional £9.0M, or 2.5%. This suggests a significant commitment to external providers for essential environmental services. Other key suppliers include LANCASHIRE CC - PRESTON COUNTY HALL, a public sector partner, with £16.8M (4.7%), and BURNLEY LEISURE, likely a local trust or arm's length body, receiving £12.9M (3.6%). These contracts represent core public services and require clear performance indicators to demonstrate their value to the community.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council about the detailed scope, performance metrics, and cost-benefit analysis of contracts with LIBERATA UK LTD or MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS LTD.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to hear discussions and decisions regarding major contracts and spending proposals.
  • Check the council's published contracts register for more in-depth information on high-value agreements and their terms.
  • Write to your local councillor about concerns regarding the value for money of large contracts or the concentration of spending with a few key suppliers.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Burnley Borough Council yourself and conduct your own analysis.
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
